title: What an American thinks about Mexico

transcript.text: All right what an American thinks about Mexico I can't believe I never got around
To doing this video okay my experiences with Mexico I've been to Mexico three
Times I went there for ten days back in like 1984 or something like that 1982
Or 1980 at Puerto Vallarta and they were friendly and I saw like crabs all over
The beaches and then I went there again I rode my bike from Austin Texas down to Mexico City
Mexico the roads are really that's are more narrow there's no they don't have they don't
Have shoulders on their roads so it's sketchy for a bike at one point I was really freaked
Out because um because I was riding down the road and there are these really steep
Reeds and there's all these cars passing me really fast and I didn't have any room to
Get over because the reeds were going up they would hit my handlebar but there was no um
There was no uh shoulder so I was freaked out when I finally got through that because people
Were passing me really fast not give me any room but um um as far as the Mexican people um
They seem kind of indifferent like to me as an American they were just like they'd seen like
Plenty of Americans before but they didn't I didn't have any experiences with you know Mexicans
Being rude to me or anything they were friendly people um although I got robbed a lot by needs
Um I was in a hotel room in Mexico City for like four days I was really sick and when I left I
Realized that they had taken a bunch of my stuff I had lots of like little things you know and they'd
Yeah they snagged a bunch of stuff happened in Honduras too um and uh my bike was stolen too
I climbed this mountain right outside of Mexico City and I locked it with a really cheap lock because
I like lost my lock I can't I can't remember how I lost it maybe the maids took it but I bought
Another lock but it was a really cheap lock and uh the Mexicans uh they cut my lock while I was
Climbing the mountain I came back my bike was gone and they did they did actually overcharge me
Um as an American because I was riding down the road and my uh my bike rack cracked so I had to
Get it um soldered and there was a solder guy right there and he did this solder thing which took like
Five minutes and they charged me 15 bucks so that wasn't so bad but uh there's lots of Mexico City
Itself is one of the most fascinating cities I've ever been to I thought it was beautiful because
There is uh they have their main plaza which is huge it's biggest plaza I'd ever seen gigantic
Plaza um and but they didn't have any straight performers or anything in there it was kind of
Deserted people would just use it to walk across it there was a McDonald's next to it but on the
Southern end of the plaza there is this building which I guess was one of the original buildings of
The Mexican country but you could see it was sideways it had sunk in because Mexico City used
To be on a lake and they built those old buildings and before they the lake had totally drained out
And yeah I saw it sideways and then right next to that there was a big cathedral and right next to
That was the um the ruins of an old Aztec pyramid which I looked at that was pretty cool that was a
A museum and then a few blocks away was another Aztec pyramid that I went and looked at so they yeah
They've got the Aztec pyramid scattered around Mexico City um and uh my hotel was like right there next to
The plaza but there is this uh this road Avenida something or other I think it's called maybe Avenida
Del Sol but it goes from the plaza all the way over to this big hill which has this this big um
Castle like palace on top of which I guess is where the president lived but um it's a straight shot
And a really wide road from that hill all the way to the plaza and it was really pretty
And there's a bike path along it um and next to that is this big um like park that's gigantic
Like a big um big forest with really wide roads but no cars can go in there and people selling stuff
I guess on Saturdays it's a market but uh and then they had little trails that go off so it was a cool
Little uh park within the city on the southern side of the city they still have the canals from
The ancient lake but I didn't look at that I should have the city has when I was there back in 2000 99
No no 2000 oh my god 12 years ago it was um had so many volkswagen vans I mean bugs green volkswagen
Bugs painted green and white at the stop signs I guess where they hang out to go pick up the cab
They had like 50 of them I got a picture of it just all packed in there like that and a lot of the
Little buses little mini buses people would take were uh were volkswagen vans but they were the old
Model the round kind but you could tell that they were um they were built just a few years ago because
Because the grills were plastic and uh yeah they had all those all over the place that's kind of
Interesting maybe the bugs were the same way I heard they had a factory an old you know volkswagen
Factory that was still there um making the old ones but uh let's see what else I didn't meet any
Mexicans who could speak English their English wasn't so good one really interesting thing about
Mexico is that uh Mexico City itself is really high um it's like 7,000 feet so the weather there
Is not like you would expect it to be it's not like because it's so far south it's not really hot
It's really crispy and warm and cool and um not jungly and um but the I rode my bike across and when
You ride your bike across over the course of 11 days across the country you can you notice things
That somebody who drives a car doesn't because they don't actually get to look at the people
I noticed that Mexico City itself had a lot more Spanish blood European blooded people there
Than the rest of Mexico the rest of Mexico um who I would the people Mexicans I would interact with
Looked like had they had a heavy Indian Native American blood there were the dark skin shorter
Stouter um but then I went to Mexico City and all of a sudden all over the place there was like
Span Spanish looking people all over the place it's like a totally different country it was really
Kind of shocking um and also all most of Mexico is jungle I'd riding my bike down uh you know right
Next to the Gulf of Mexico there on the road and I look over and it'd be jungle like you couldn't even
Walk through it so tightly densely packed but um but then I rode my bike up to Mexico City one day and
It was like up the mountainside the whole time and um and I could see how the jungle and the vines went away
And then next thing I know it's pine forests and uh it's like it's like you're in like Colorado you know
Or like Utah or something alpine desert not the jungle like the rest of Mexico so uh yeah that's a different
Different and then I went back down to uh the Yucatan and it was jungly again but um but the Mexicans
Who I met who I interacted with I didn't hear any of them speak English and it seemed really poor too
Some of them on the side of the road like they would their bath bathroom would be like an outhouse
A separate a couple of houses were like that stores that I got like their store and their house
Were the same thing same building um and uh I went to one town it had cobblestone streets oh and the
Cars that they drove they had uh big American cars from the 70s and they're uh like not in Mexico City
But the other town I think Veracruz their taxis were big 70s Dodges and stuff Fords yeah that's that
They weren't particularly outgoing I didn't really chat with so many of them I was just
Paying attention minding my own business riding my bike on the side of the road they had these burritos
Which were different from American they're a lot more uh greasier than American burritos but they're
Good and they had a lot of those like little tamale wraps on the side of the road yeah it's a cool place
To visit so that's what an American thinks about Mexico
